title: Velvety Algerian Flax Eggs Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful tagine dish featuring flax eggs, perfect for a hearty breakfast or brunch. The earthy and velvety texture of the flax eggs pairs beautifully with the aromatic spices of the tagine.
instructions: Combine ground flaxseed and water in a bowl and stir to form a gel-like mixture, set aside to thicken for 5 minutes.
Heat the olive oil in a large tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the chopped carrots and cook for 5 minutes, or until they begin to soften.
Stir in the cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chickpeas, flaxseed mixture, and chicken broth to the tagine, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20-25 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Stir in the chopped almonds and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until heated through.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze leftovers for up to 3 days, or freeze for up to 2 months.
To reheat, stir in a little water or broth if the tagine has thickened too much.
